title: The Impact of Iran's Actions on Global Oil Prices and Shipping
content:
Fears of a global economic shock spread widely after a crucial Middle Eastern shipping route was effectively shut off. The sudden closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway through which one-fifth of the world’s oil travels, paralyzed global trade. Following drone and missile attacks on regional energy infrastructure, military advisers warned that any passing ships would be targeted. Consequently, thousands of vessels, representing a significant percentage of global shipping tonnage, were left stranded in the Persian Gulf.

The attacks had an immediate and severe impact on regional and international energy operations. At least five oil tankers were struck by projectiles, forcing top natural gas exporters to suspend production and intercept incoming threats. Oil and gas prices surged globally, prompting serious concerns about rising inflation and slowing industrial productivity. Experts noted that resolving the crisis depended heavily on whether international forces could quickly secure and reopen the vital waterway. To head off a prolonged energy crisis, government officials declared they would provide military escorts to ensure the free flow of energy.
